title: Size selection (Purification)
steps: Calculate the amount of magnetic beads you need and DISTRIBUTE in a new 1.5 mL eppendorf tube ( Mix the bottle VIGOROUSLY to make sure it's homogeneous ). For example, if you have 5 samples to clean up, distribute 49.5 μl in a new 1.5 mL eppendorf tube. %s %s SampleMagnetic beads needMagnetic beads need *1.119 μl9.9 μl218 μl19.8 μl327 μl29.7 μl436 μl39.6 μl545 μl49.5 μl654 μl59.4 μl763 μl69.3 μl872 μl79.2 μl The ratio is 20μl of sample use 9μl of magnetic beads to clean up (0.45X to keep long DNA fragments). Magnetic bead : BeaverBeads™ DNA Select Isolation
Mix sample and beads by gently flicking the tube for 5mins 00:05:00 Be gentle, mix too aggressive will make DNA fragmented
Transfer the tube to the 8-strip magnetic rack. After all the magnetic beads are arrested, remove the supernatant.
Add 200 µL 80% ethanol, flip the magnetic rack around to clean the pellet. Wait until all the magnetic beads are arrested, remove the supernatant. FRESHLY make 80% ethanol
Flash spin the tube, and put on the magnetic rack. Remove ANY trace of liquid residuals using 10 μl pipette. Caution: DO NOT let the beads crack
Add 10 µL elution buffer. Mix gently by flicking the tube and make sure all magnetic beads are dissolved in buffer, then flash spin down the sample.
Transfer the tube to the magnetic rack. After all the magnetic beads are arrested, collect the 10 µL supernatant to a 200 μl PCR tube or a 8-strip PCR tube.
The clean-up sample is now ready for 2' PCR.
Incubate the tube in PCR thermal cycler using " 37_incubation " program for 10min 00:10:00 .
In each 20 µL sample, add 9 µL magnetic beads in. Pipetting thoroughly every time before adding to make sure all magnetic beads mixed well.
creator: Yin-Tse Huang
